source.title: Theta-band phase locking during encoding leads to coordinated entorhinal-hippocampal replay

confidence_rationale: Direct evidence that theta-band phase-locking during encoding predicts which dMEC cells subsequently participate in coordinated HC replay, with experience-dependent growth of the synchrony and task acquisition. MECH-269 relies on MECH-089 (theta-gamma nesting feeds the per-cycle summary the proposer anchors on); Santos-Pata et al. show that theta-locked cortical ensembles preferentially join hippocampal replay trajectories, which is the expected behaviour if anchor selection reads theta-cycle-averaged summaries. The experience-dependent increase in synchrony with learning is additional support: the selection process is not static but tracks the agent's acquisition of the task, consistent with V_s being a running alignment signal that improves with experience.
failure_signatures: If theta-band phase-locking during encoding did not predict subsequent replay participation, MECH-269's reliance on theta-cycle-averaged summaries (via MECH-089) as the substrate for anchor selection would lose biological grounding. If the dMEC-CA1 synchrony had been static rather than experience-dependent, the running-alignment structure MECH-269 assumes for V_s would be weakened -- V_s should be learning-sensitive, and biological coordination should be learning-sensitive too, and Santos-Pata et al. show it is.

mapping.source_claim_statement: A subset of deep-medial-entorhinal-cortex cells that are preferentially locked to hippocampal theta-band LFP oscillations during movement are preferentially coordinated with hippocampal replay during offline periods. dMEC synchrony with CA1 replay peaks approximately 10ms after replay initiation in CA1, suggesting extra-hippocampal information propagation during offline periods. Theta-modulated dMEC cells show a striking experience-dependent increase in synchronisation with hippocampal replay trajectories, mirroring task acquisition.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-269 anchor selection consumes per-stream theta-cycle-averaged summaries (inherited from MECH-089) rather than raw instantaneous latent values. Santos-Pata et al. provide evidence that theta-phase-locked ensembles in dMEC are the ones whose activity participates in coordinated CA1 replay; that is, the biological substrate for replay content is theta-structured. This matches MECH-269's design commitment to theta-cycle-summary inputs. The experience-dependence of the synchrony is the additional support: V_s in MECH-269 is a running alignment score that should strengthen with task acquisition, and the Santos-Pata finding that dMEC-CA1 synchrony strengthens with experience is the biological analogue of that.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Santos-Pata et al. study dMEC and CA1 during spatial task acquisition; they do not isolate per-stream alignment. The content being synchronised is cell assemblies for spatial trajectories, not REE's z_world / z_harm_s / z_goal streams. The mapping from theta-phase-locked encoding ensembles to stream-local V_s readout is a REE-side commitment. The paper supports the architectural role of theta-phase-locking in selecting which ensembles participate in replay; it does not support the specific stream-local decomposition MECH-269 asserts.
mapping.source_context: Rats learning a novel spatial task; simultaneous CA1 and deep-medial-entorhinal-cortex recordings; analysis of dMEC spike phase-locking to CA1 theta LFP during movement and of dMEC-CA1 synchrony during subsequent offline replay epochs; experience-dependent analysis across training.

confidence_components.notes: Curr Biol paper, Olafsdottir senior author (the 2018 review already in the MECH-269 evidence set), rigorous simultaneous dMEC-CA1 electrophysiology with experience-dependent analysis. Mapping fidelity is 0.70 because the paper directly supports the theta-phase-locking-to-replay-participation architecture MECH-269 depends on (via MECH-089), and because the experience-dependence matches the running-alignment character of V_s. Transfer risk moderate: the architectural lesson (theta-phase-locking selects replay participants) transfers; the REE-specific stream-local V_s claim remains an extrapolation.
